commands.repair_reason: This external draft PR has no narrow code repair finding for automation; it needs maintainer review of route-inheritance risk plus contributor-supplied real TUI behavior proof.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the diff changes Gateway delivery/transcript handling without new dependencies, workflows, permissions, secrets, or network surfaces. commands.review_summary:
The PR updates Gateway chat routing, source-reply payload metadata preservation, media/transcript mirror handling, tests, and TUI docs so TUI/WebChat internal source replies are broadcast as chat finals and mirrored safely.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main source shows TUI is not included in the WebChat no-inherit/internal-source-surface path, and the PR body supplies a before-failing focused regression test; I did not rerun tests because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: This external draft PR has no narrow code repair finding for automation; it needs maintainer review of route-inheritance risk plus contributor-supplied real TUI behavior proof.

target.title: fix(gateway): deliver internal source replies to TUI
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: TUI-originated chat runs could miss message-tool source replies because the Gateway treated only WebChat as an internal source surface.
- Solution: Treat TUI/WebChat as internal source surfaces, broadcast message-tool source replies back over Gateway chat events, and keep transcript mirrors aligned.
- What changed: Preserved source-reply metadata through reply normalization/media handling/TTS, added source-reply broadcast and transcript mirror upgrades, and kept sensitive source media live-only.
- What did NOT change: External channel delivery policy, provider auth, and normal Pi assistant-turn persistence.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #38957
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

TUI users should see the same visible source reply that the agent sends through the message tool. The previous route/source-surface split let the reply exist in other surfaces or transcript machinery while the active TUI chat stream saw no final reply.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: TUI-originated `chat.send` source replies no longer disappear from the TUI chat event stream when an agent uses the message tool for the visible source reply.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw Gateway test harness with a real WebSocket client registered as client id `openclaw-tui` and mode `ui`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts -t "delivers message-tool source replies to TUI clients over gateway chat events"`
Evidence after fix: Terminal capture from the command above: `Test Files 1 passed (1); Tests 1 passed | 24 skipped (25)`. The captured TUI WebSocket event had `runId=idem-tui-source-reply:internal-source-reply:0`, `sessionKey=agent:main:main`, `state=final`, and message text `Summary visible in the TUI.`
Observed result after fix: The source reply was delivered over the TUI chat event stream and associated with the active TUI session instead of being invisible to that client.
What was not tested: A live provider call and an interactive `pnpm tui` terminal session were not used for PR-head proof; the tested boundary is the Gateway/TUI WebSocket delivery path with controlled agent dispatch.
Before evidence: The same TUI delivery regression test failed against `origin/main` with `missing TUI source reply; seen=[]` when only the test was applied.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: Gateway route resolution and source-reply replay treated WebChat as the internal source surface but did not include the TUI client identity (`openclaw-tui`, mode `ui`).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No Gateway/TUI WebSocket test asserted that message-tool source replies are delivered to TUI clients.
- Contributing context: Source-reply metadata was lost by several normalization/media/TTS transformations, so rich/media source replies could not be reliably mirrored or replayed.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Seam / integration test
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts` and `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: a TUI WebSocket client receives a final chat event for a message-tool internal source reply, including media and sensitive-media mirror handling.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug sits at the Gateway/client event boundary, not inside an external provider.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none before this PR.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

TUI users now see message-tool source replies in the active chat stream. Sensitive media in source replies remains live-only and is not prewritten into durable transcript mirrors.

## Diagram

```text
Before:
TUI chat.send -> agent message tool source reply -> no TUI chat final event

After:
TUI chat.send -> agent message tool source reply -> Gateway source-reply final event -> TUI sees reply
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux local checkout
- Runtime/container: Node 22.22.2 via repository test wrapper
- Model/provider: mocked agent dispatch in Gateway test harness
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway TUI/WebSocket client path
- Relevant config (redacted): default test config

### Steps

1. Applied the TUI source-reply regression test to `origin/main`; it failed with `missing TUI source reply; seen=[]`.
2. Ran the same test after this patch.
3. Ran the focused dispatch/Gateway/media checks listed below.

### Expected

TUI receives a final chat event for the internal source reply, while durable transcript mirrors avoid raw `MEDIA:` paths and sensitive data URLs.

### Actual

The TUI WebSocket event was emitted with the source-reply run id and visible summary text; sensitive source media remained out of the transcript file.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: TUI source-reply Gateway event delivery, source-reply media mirror upgrade, sensitive source media mirror suppression, metadata preservation through normalization/media/TTS, and route non-inheritance for TUI/WebChat source surfaces.
- Edge cases checked: image-only source replies, delayed source mirrors, dropped media, TTS synthesis, staged audio trust, and sensitive data-image replies.
- What you did **not** verify: live provider invocation and an interactive terminal TUI session on PR head.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: The source-reply path now creates an additional Gateway final event for internal UI surfaces.
  - Mitigation: Uses distinct source-reply run ids and tests ensure normal external route behavior is unchanged.

## Verification

- `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts -t "delivers message-tool source replies to TUI clients over gateway chat events"`
- `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat-reply-media.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/reply-media-paths.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile /tmp/openclaw-test-src-tui-proof7.tsbuildinfo --pretty false`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check docs/web/tui.md src/auto-reply/reply/normalize-reply.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-media-paths.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-media-paths.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat-reply-media.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat-reply-media.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts src/utils/message-channel.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main`
- `codex review --base origin/main` (clean: no discrete correctness issues found)
